name: Gosport East

links.title: Gosport Police - Twitter
links.description: Our Gosport Police Twitter account started in April 2012. We now have nearly 6,000 people following us. Daily updates are shared on this account.

links.title: Gosport Police Facebook
links.description: The Gosport Police Facebook page started in March 2020. Since then, our following has grown. We now have 14,000 people following us. Please check out, like and follow the Facebook page to keep on top of everything your local Gosport teams are doing. It's updated several times a day with details of arrests, charges, beat surgeries, witness appeals and lots more.

locations.name: Gosport Neighbourhood Police Office
locations.address: Gosport Town Hall, High Street, Gosport

description:
The Safer Neighbourhood Team for the Privett and Alverstoke Neighbourhood area is currently made up a Sergeant, Police Constables and Police Community Support Officers (PCSOs).

The East Neighbourhood has a very diverse demographic, from the more affluent area of Alverstoke to the more deprived area of Leesland. The Leesland ward features within the list of most deprived areas of the country. The current estimated population is around 18,096 with the number of households estimated at 7816.

The neighbourhood attracts many visitors mainly to the Stokes Bay area which is a picturesque stretch of coastline well supported by hotels, cafes and restaurants. This area can become particularly busy during the summer months with sporadic incidents of anti social behaviour reported. The area also has many large areas of park land which are also well attended by the community which again and lead to issues necessitating police attention.
